system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      T;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 0 0 1 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (10 0 0);
        value           uniform (10 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Back face (z = -0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0        -0.005 )   // 0
    (  0.3048   0        -0.005 )   // 1
    (  0.3048   0.1524   -0.005 )   // 2
    ( -0.15242  0.1524   -0.005 )   // 3

    // Front face (z = 0.005)
    ( -0.15242  0         0.005 )   // 4
    (  0.3048   0         0.005 )   // 5
    (  0.3048   0.1524    0.005 )   // 6
    ( -0.15242  0.1524    0.005 )   // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (100 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (4 7 6 5)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Face Already Assigned to Multiple Patches

**Error Message:**
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(0 3 7 4) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch. This is face 0 of patch 3 named bottom.
```

**Diagnosis:**

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict` after Attempt 3's changes, the situation is:

Vertices:
- 0 = (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1 = (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2 = (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 3 = (-0.15242, 0.1524, -0.005)
- 4 = (-0.15242, 0, 0.005)
- 5 = (0.3048, 0, 0.005)
- 6 = (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)
- 7 = (-0.15242, 0.1524, 0.005)

Block: `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)`

Current boundary assignments:
- **`inlet`**: `(0 4 7 3)` — left face at x=-0.15242 ✓
- **`outlet`**: `(1 2 6 5)` — right face at x=+0.3048 ✓
- **`top`**: `(3 7 6 2)` — top face at y=+0.1524 ✓
- **`bottom`**: `(0 3 7 4)` — x-z plane at y=0 ✗ **CONFLICT**

The face `(0 3 7 4)` is listed under `bottom`, but it is **already assigned to the `inlet` patch** as `(0 4 7 3)`. In OpenFOAM's `blockMesh`, when listing multiple patches, each unique face must appear exactly once across ALL patches. The face `(0 3 7 4)` and `(0 4 7 3)` are the SAME face — they differ only in vertex winding order, but OpenFOAM treats them as identical and detects the duplication.

**Root Cause:** The face `(0 3 7 4)` (x-z plane at y=0) is being assigned to BOTH `inlet` AND `bottom`. It cannot be in two patches simultaneously.

**Proposed Solution:**

Since the user specifies "inlet velocity is 10 m/s", the **true inlet** is at x = -0.15242 (the left face), which is `(0 4 7 3)`. Keep that as `inlet`. The face at y=0 (the x-z plane, `(0 3 7 4`)**) should be assigned to `bottom` but **removed** from `inlet`. Simply delete the face from the `inlet` patch and add it to `bottom`:

- **`inlet`**: keep only `(0 4 7 3)`
- **`bottom`**: change from `(0 3 7 4)` to **(0 3 7 4)** — wait, that's the same face. The issue is that `(0 4 7 3)` and `(0 3 7 4)` are the same geometric face with different winding orders.

Both patches cannot share the same face. The solution is to **remove the face from one patch entirely**. Since the user requires an inlet at x=-0.15242, keep it in `inlet`. Move the y=0 face to `bottom`:

- **`inlet`**: `{ type patch; faces ( (0 4 7 3) ); }`
- **`bottom`**: `{ type symmetryPlane; faces ( (0 3 7 4) ); }`

Wait — these ARE the same face with different winding. The real issue is that the face `(0 3 7 4)` is being interpreted as the same entity as `(0 4 7 3)`. The solution is to **only assign it to one patch**. Choose `inlet` for this face since it carries the velocity specification. Then what is `bottom`?

Actually, re-reading the error carefully: the face **currently assigned to `bottom`** is `(0 3 7 4)`, and the error says this face is "already belongs to some other patch". Looking at the current config, `inlet` has `(0 4 7 3)`. These are the same face. So the fix is:

**Remove `(0 3 7 4)` from the `bottom` patch** — leave `bottom` as an empty patch with no faces, OR better yet, **remove the `bottom` patch entirely** since the y=0 plane at x=-0.15242 to x=0.3048 is not a free surface — it's the domain floor. But since `inlet` already takes that face, `bottom` should have **no faces**.

Better approach: **Delete the `bottom` patch entirely** (empty it) and let it become an unused face group. Or rename/reassign properly.

**Simplest fix:** Remove the face from `bottom` so it has no faces, OR better yet, **swap the face definitions so they don't overlap**. The face at y=0 is `(0 3 7 4)` which equals `(0 4 7 3)` in reverse. Since `inlet` already has `(0 4 7 3)`, `bottom` cannot also have `(0 3 7 4)`. Either:
1. Give `bottom` an empty face list, OR
2. Redefine `inlet` to use a DIFFERENT face and give `bottom` the y=0 face — but there is no other face for `inlet` since the left face IS `(0 4 7 3)/(0 3 7 4)`.

**Conclusion:** The `bottom` patch must be **emptied** (no faces assigned) since the only face at y=0 is already used by `inlet`. Alternatively, restructure so `inlet` is something else — but with only 4 faces per side of a hex block, there simply aren't enough distinct faces. The cleanest fix is to **remove the `bottom` patch entry entirely** (make it empty) since the y=0 plane face is already claimed by `inlet`.

## Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cascading from blockMesh failure

Same resolution — fix blockMesh first.

## Summary

**Change `blockMeshDict`**: Empty the `bottom` patch (remove its face list) since the face at y=0 is already assigned to `inlet`. The `bottom` entry should be:

```
bottom
{
    type wall;
    faces ();
}
```

Or simply remove the `bottom` patch entry altogether. All other files should remain unchanged.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'In the bottom patch, replace the face definition (0 3 7 4) with an empty face list so bottom becomes: { type wall; faces (); } to avoid duplicating the face that is already assigned to inlet'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
